author: Steve Griffing PE(CSE) CISSP-ISSEP CPP
rev_n_summary: Rev N is the first fully-documented 24-inch build of the Serenity-class tiltrotor UAV. It supersedes the 18-inch Rev M design. Key changes: scale 18→24 in, propulsion 4×80mm→2×(2×50mm in series) tandem nacelles, counter-rotating EDF pairs (port CW / starboard CCW) for zero net torque, 8-fin twisted stators integrated into each nacelle print (CF-PETG), gear-linked iris nozzles on both nacelles driven passively by the tilt pivot (no dedicated nozzle servo), single servo for rear fuselage 120mm nozzle, nacelle tilt range 0°(cruise)→120°(backing) with hard stops at −5°/140°. Avionics: 8× PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254) + Cape-A/B unchanged from Rev M. Rev N intake change: belly scoop removed; 4 radial scoop inlets (90° spacing, dorsal/port/ventral/stbd) cut into hull at neck station ~310mm from nose; CF-PETG structural intake frame bonded into each opening; cross-shaped PETG plenum manifold routes air from all 4 scoops to the 120mm EDF fan face inside Panel F engine bell. SiK 915MHz belly antenna relocated forward to station ~260mm.

hull_geometry.fill: 2 lb/ft³ (32 kg/m³) two-part closed-cell polyurethane foam, 4× expansion
hull_geometry.nacelle_od: ~60.5 mm (X) × 67 mm (Y) — bore inscribed ID ≈ 55.5 mm
hull_geometry.nacelle_length: 148.3 mm (Z axis, Z=0 = nozzle exit face)

print_schedule.desc: Fuselage mid-section — canonical Serenity belly geometry, NO belly scoop. Replaces s_middle_intake_shell24.stl for Rev N 4-radial-intake build.
print_schedule.notes: Generate STL from SCAD with OpenSCAD CLI: openscad -o s_middle_canonical_shell24.stl serenity/stl/s_middle_canonical_shell24.scad

print_schedule.desc: Rear fuselage / engine bell section with 4 radial scoop windows at neck station (~310mm from nose). 4 rectangular cutouts (65mm wide × 60mm axial) at 90° spacing (dorsal/port/ventral/stbd). Canonical shell geometry otherwise preserved.
print_schedule.notes: Generate STL from SCAD. Verify scoop-window station alignment in slicer before printing; adjust NECK_X parameter if needed.

print_schedule.desc: CF-PETG structural intake frame ring — bonds into 4 scoop windows at neck station. Provides aerodynamic intake lips, registration tongues (5mm), bonding shoulders (5mm), and internal duct arms routing air toward plenum. Replaces structural stiffness lost by cutting hull openings.
print_schedule.notes: Print with hardened-steel nozzle. Bond with structural epoxy (e.g. West System 105/206) into hull scoop cutouts. Cure 24h before foam-fill.

print_schedule.desc: PETG 4-to-1 cross-shaped plenum manifold — collects air from 4 intake frame duct arms (station ~310mm) and delivers to 120mm EDF fan face (station ~430mm). Rectangular arms (65×60mm) taper to 120mm circular EDF outlet over 80mm transition length.
print_schedule.notes: Bond aft end to EDF mount ring; bond forward arm ends to intake frame duct arm exits. Seal all joints with epoxy fillet. Pressure-test with shop vac before closing hull.

print_schedule.desc: Port nacelle with integrated CW stator (SWIRL_DIR=+1) — generated by blender_nacelle_integrated_v1.py
print_schedule.notes: EDF bore ID≈55.5mm; stator fins Z=53..73mm; print with hardened-steel nozzle

print_schedule.desc: Starboard nacelle with integrated CCW stator (SWIRL_DIR=−1) — generated by blender_nacelle_integrated_v1.py
print_schedule.notes: Mirror image of port nacelle; CCW stator cancels port torque reaction

print_schedule.desc: Pivot arm — carries tilt servo pushrod
Nacelle iris nozzle petal (50mm EDF, hull-matched outer surface)
print_schedule.notes: 8 per nacelle × 2 nacelles; print flat; inner concave face print in translucent-blue PETG for LED backlight effect

print_schedule.desc: Nacelle iris base ring (fixed, seats at nozzle exit face)
Rear fuselage iris nozzle petal (120mm EDF, 38° span)
print_schedule.notes: Inner face: translucent-blue PETG for LED backlight

print_schedule.desc: Rear nozzle base ring with 8 fixed ribs (one-piece cone frame, visible at all petal states)
Landing gear feet (×4 in one STL)

nacelle_edfs.desc: 50mm EDF unit @ 6S — 4 total (2 per nacelle, tandem series)
nacelle_edfs.config: 2 per nacelle in tandem/series — single shared bore, Z=5..50mm (EDF2 downstream) and Z=76..126mm (EDF1 upstream)
nacelle_edfs.rotation: Port nacelle: CW from intake | Starboard nacelle: CCW from intake (counter-rotating pair for zero net torque)
nacelle_edfs.notes: Verify rotation direction before installing in nacelle. Spin-test on bench first.

nacelle_escs.desc: 40A 6S BLHeli32 ESC with BDSHOT telemetry — one per 50mm EDF
nacelle_escs.notes: ESC1/ESC2 in port nacelle hub bore; ESC3/ESC4 in starboard. Route leads through integrated hub bore (ID=16mm).

nacelle_stators.desc: 8-fin twisted inter-stage stator — integrated into nacelle shell print (not a separate part)
nacelle_stators.notes: Generated by blender_nacelle_integrated_v1.py. No separate stator_50mm.stl required for Rev N.

rear_edf.desc: 120mm EDF unit @ 6S — rear fuselage inside engine bell, exhaust straight aft
rear_edf.location: Panel F engine bell, fan face at station ~430mm from nose. Axis aligned with fuselage centreline, intake forward (+Z toward nose).
rear_edf.intake: 4 radial scoops at station ~310mm via s_neck_intake_frame + s_aft_edf_plenum (replaces belly scoop).
rear_edf.notes: Fan annular area ≈10,683mm²; combined scoop capture area = 15,600mm² (ratio 1.46× — exceeds 1.3× minimum for duct velocity matching). Verify EDF ring / plenum outlet concentricity before bonding.

thrust_summary.dry_mass_g: ~2800 (estimated — hull prints + avionics + motors + servos, excl. battery)
thrust_summary.notes: Lower T/W than 18-inch design due to larger hull mass at 24-inch scale. Adequate for stable hover and cargo ops.

nacelle_tilt_servos.desc: Nacelle tilt servo — fuselage mounted, one per nacelle
nacelle_tilt_servos.type: Digital, metal gear (e.g. DS3218MG class or equivalent ≥25 kg·cm at 6V)
nacelle_tilt_servos.notes: Mount in fuselage bays adjacent to wing root. Drives nacelle tilt via pushrod to pivot arm (s_pivot_arm_a_scaled24.stl). Gear ratio through pivot arm sized for full range in servo's 0–180° travel.

nozzle_gear_linkage.desc: Passive mechanical linkage — nacelle nozzle driven by tilt pivot, no dedicated servo
nozzle_gear_linkage.mechanism: Sector gear (R=22mm, fixed to tilt bracket) → Drive pinion (R=6mm, on nacelle body) → 1:1 bevel pair (90° axis redirect) → Crown pinion (R=6mm) → Nozzle inner ring rack (R=28mm)
nozzle_gear_linkage.nozzle_state: 0° nacelle → nozzle fully closed (hull-matched petal surface); 90° nacelle → nozzle ~71° ring rotation = fully open
nozzle_gear_linkage.nozzle_120deg: At 120° nacelle, nozzle ring would be over-driven — internal hard stop in ring gear limits ring to 71° max.
nozzle_gear_linkage.ref: serenity/artifacts/nacelle-nozzle-gear.jsx for full gear spec and visualization

pivot_bearings.desc: MF104ZZ flanged bearing 4×10×4mm — nacelle pivot (2 per nacelle)

pivot_rod.desc: 4mm OD solid carbon-fibre pivot rod, cut to length per pivot housing drawing

sector_gear.desc: M0.5 sector gear R=22mm, integral to tilt bracket — resin print or CNC

bevel_pair.desc: M0.5 bevel gear pair 1:1, 90° axis redirect

pinion.desc: M0.5 drive pinion R=6mm, mounts on nacelle outer shell

nacelle_nozzles.desc: 2× iris nozzles (one per nacelle) — gear-driven passively from tilt pivot, NO dedicated servo
nacelle_nozzles.hinge_pins: 3mm diameter × 5mm stainless steel, one per petal
nacelle_nozzles.link_ring: Piano wire 0.8mm bent to link ring connecting petals to nozzle inner ring
nacelle_nozzles.petal_material: PETG body + translucent-blue PETG inner face (print as two-colour or paint)

rear_nozzle.petals: 8 moving petals + 8 fixed ribs (integral to rear_nozzle_frame.stl)
rear_nozzle.hinge_pins: 3mm diameter × 5mm stainless steel, one per petal

servo.desc: Rear nozzle servo — SG90 class or equivalent (small load)
servo.notes: Mount inside rear fuselage section adjacent to 120mm EDF duct. Piano wire pushrod to nozzle ring.

hinge_pins_total.desc: 3mm OD × 5mm stainless steel roll pin or solid pin
hinge_pins_total.notes: 16 for nacelle petals (8×2) + 8 for rear petals

led_rings.desc: WS2812B LED ring (addressable RGB) — inside each nozzle duct for backlight effect
led_rings.notes: One per nozzle (2 nacelle + 1 rear). Inner petal face printed in translucent-blue PETG; LED ring mounts at duct exit lip.

avionics.note: Avionics hardware identical to Rev M. All 8 PocketBeagle 2 Industrial (AM6254) nodes with Cape-A (FC nodes) and Cape-B (CN nodes). See bom_revM.json for full avionics part-level detail.

nodes.desc: Cape-B PCB 90×60mm — Comms + Logging + Payload cape

RCRS-49 sub-module PCB — 49MHz TDDS RC. Antenna: top wire running dorsal hull spine from nose post (~120mm) to EDF cone post (~600mm), ~470mm wire length, base-loaded (38uH coil + LC pi-net at nose post), CF keel bar counterpoise. No belly SMA required.

bay_assignments.bay_A: CN1 (Cape-B, lower) + FC1 (Cape-A, upper) — nose section, ~X=0..91mm
bay_assignments.bay_E: CN4 (Cape-B, lower) + FC4 (Cape-A, upper) — aft service, ~X=320..388mm

antenna_system.desc: 49 MHz top wire — 0.3 mm stainless steel, ~470 mm, dorsal spine
antenna_system.notes: End-fed shortened wire; horizontal polarisation; cross-pol loss ~6–12 dB at ≤300 m acceptable

antenna_system.desc: Forward wire post + base-loading coil — PETG post + 38 uH coil + LC pi-net at dorsal hull ~120 mm from nose
antenna_system.search: 38uH air-core inductor + variable cap + PETG printed post
antenna_system.notes: 3 mm clearance from GPS patch face; bench-test HDOP ≤1.5 with RCRS TX active before first flight

antenna_system.desc: Aft wire post + ceramic bead insulator — top of rear nozzle cone ~600 mm from nose
antenna_system.notes: Strain-relief wire at aft post; open-circuit (insulated) termination

battery_hover.notes: Primary flight battery. C-rating ≥15C continuous for peak draw (~60A).

battery_cargo.notes: Use when payload ≤250g and flight time can be reduced.

distribution.avionics_bus: 5V BEC from main bus — 8 PB2-I nodes + capes + servos
distribution.servo_bus: 6V regulated from 6S BEC — nacelle tilt servos + rear nozzle servo
distribution.led_bus: 5V from BEC — WS2812B LED rings (3× nozzle backlight)
distribution.bec_spec: ≥5A dual BEC (5V + 6V output) on main power distribution board

wiring.main_leads: 10AWG silicone — battery to PDB and ESC inputs
wiring.esc_motor: 16AWG silicone — each ESC to EDF motor
wiring.signal: 28AWG shielded twisted pair for servo signal + DSHOT leads
wiring.data_bus: PTFE 3mm ID conduit, full hull length, for CAN FD / RS-485 / 1553 / ETH

keel.notes: Runs full hull length; datum marks at 91, 165, 251, 320, 388mm from nose

spars.length_mm: 2× 350mm (wing spar through wing root to fuselage)
spars.notes: Carries nacelle tilt loads. Bending stress at max nacelle torque (~0.13 Nm) ≈ 18 MPa — well below CF allowable (600 MPa).

ring_frames.desc: 2mm CF plate, 5 station profiles (91, 165, 251, 320, 388mm from nose)

foam_fill.spec: 2 lb/ft³ (32 kg/m³) two-part closed-cell polyurethane, 4× expansion ratio
foam_fill.volume_l: ~0.7 L mixed (fills hull void after EPS void formers placed for bays + cargo)
foam_fill.notes: Provides structural rigidity, crash absorption, and near-neutral buoyancy. Do NOT foam the nacelle interior.

cargo.door: Clamshell — N20 winch motor + auto-latch cradle
cargo.winch: N20 300RPM 6V gear motor + HX711 load cell (Cape-B)

cost_estimate_usd.notes: Excludes one-time tools. Component prices as of 2026.
